CheckTitle: Ensure non-admin users cannot register applications
Description: Checks whether the default user role allows non-admin users to register applications. When any user can register apps, it increases the risk of rogue applications being created in the tenant.
Risk: Users who can register applications can create app registrations that request permissions, potentially tricking other users into consenting. This is a common vector for consent-grant phishing attacks. Only authorized administrators should register applications.

Remediation.Recommendation: Set 'Users can register applications' to No in the Entra ID user settings. Use the Application Developer role for users who need to create app registrations.

SourceNotes: CIS recommends restricting app registration to admins only.
